candidate_problems.text: Specify what kinds of criticism inside an LLM-containing system are genuinely sensitive to explanatory truth rather than merely correlating with human textual preferences, and identify cases where the two come apart.

Determine whether a system can generate genuinely new explanatory structure without external human critics, or whether human-supplied problem selection and criticism remain indispensable parts of the knowledge-creating architecture.
Test whether iterative self-critique by language models can reject elegant nonsense reliably enough to count as error-correction rather than style refinement.
